title: OpenClaw v2026.4.23: Image Generation, Subagents & 89 Fixes
description: OpenClaw v2026.4.23 adds OpenAI/OpenRouter image generation via Codex OAuth, subagent forked context, per-call timeoutMs, configurable local embeddings, and 89 stability fixes.
content:
# OpenClaw v2026.4.23: Image Generation, Subagents & 89 Fixes

OpenClaw v2026.4.23 shipped on April 24, 2026 with a focused but high-impact set of changes: **image generation and reference-image editing** for OpenAI and OpenRouter via Codex OAuth, **subagent forked context** for native multi-step workflows, **per-call timeout budgets** for generative media tools, and an unusually large batch of **89 bug fixes** covering every major subsystem from WhatsApp to Codex harness routing. Here is what changed and how B2B SDR teams should act on it.

---

## What Is OpenClaw?

OpenClaw is an open-source AI agent gateway that routes large language models into messaging channels — WhatsApp, Telegram, Slack, WeChat, email — with a plugin architecture for providers, tools, and multi-agent workflows. B2B export companies run it as the engine of their AI SDR agents: qualifying inbound leads, sending proposals, following up across time zones, and closing pipeline without manual intervention on every message.

The [B2B SDR Agent Template](https://github.com/iPythoning/b2b-sdr-agent-template) wraps OpenClaw with a production-ready 7-layer context system and ships with a one-line installer:

```bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/iPythoning/b2b-sdr-agent-template/main/install.sh | bash
```

---

## Key Changes in v2026.4.23

### 1. Image Generation + Reference-Image Editing (OpenAI + OpenRouter)

The OpenAI provider now supports **image generation and reference-image editing through Codex OAuth**. The same capability is extended to OpenRouter, with provider-supported quality and output format hints (resolution, style, compression).

**What this unlocks for B2B SDR teams:**

| Use case | Before v2026.4.23 | After v2026.4.23 |
|---|---|---|
| Product image in proposal | Manual export from design tool | Agent generates in-conversation |
| Personalized brochure cover | Separate creative workflow | Reference-image edit, inline |
| Voice + image combo outreach | Two separate tools | Single agent call |
| A/B visual variants | Design team request | Instant quality/format hints |

This is particularly valuable for manufacturing and industrial B2B companies where product visuals are central to every proposal. Your SDR agent can now generate a spec-accurate product render mid-conversation and attach it to the WhatsApp thread — zero design team involvement.

**Key fact:** Both providers support reference-image editing, meaning you can upload a base product image and request contextual modifications (background swap, label change, dimension callout overlay) without leaving the agent workflow.

### 2. Subagent Forked Context for Native Sessions

Agents can now launch subagents with **forked context** in native sessions. Each subagent gets its own memory slice, isolated from the parent thread, with prompt guidance and inline documentation.

**Why this matters for multi-step SDR pipelines:**

A typical enterprise B2B sales cycle involves multiple cognitive tasks that conflict in a single context window:

1. **Qualifier agent** — cold message, detect buying signals, extract company size, budget, decision timeline
2. **Researcher agent** — enrich lead data from web search, news, LinkedIn signals
3. **Closer agent** — warm follow-up, proposal generation, objection handling
4. **Scheduler agent** — meeting booking, calendar sync, CRM update

With forked context, each of these runs as an independent subagent with its own focused context — no contamination between cold qualification state and closing state. The parent agent orchestrates handoffs.

**Practical result:** Longer deal cycles handled more accurately with less prompt pollution across stages.

### 3. Per-Call `timeoutMs` for Generative Media Tools

Individual tool calls for image, video, music, and TTS generation now accept an explicit `timeoutMs`. Previously, a global timeout governed all tool calls; slow media generation could block fast text operations.

**Configuration example:**

```json
{
  "tools": {
    "image_generate": {
      "timeoutMs": 15000
    },
    "tts_generate": {
      "timeoutMs": 8000
    }
  }
}
```

For SDR agents generating voice previews or product images mid-conversation, this means tight SLA control: if image generation exceeds 15 seconds, the agent falls back gracefully without holding the entire conversation thread.

### 4. Configurable Local Embedding Context Size

Local embedding providers now accept a `contextSize` parameter (default 4096 tokens). Teams running OpenClaw on constrained infrastructure — 2 vCPU VPS, edge nodes, on-premise servers — can tune memory usage without switching to a cloud embedding provider.

```json
{
  "memory": {
    "embeddings": {
      "provider": "local",
      "contextSize": 2048
    }
  }
}
```

This is directly relevant for B2B teams in markets where cloud data residency is restricted — the local embedding path becomes viable on modest hardware.

### 5. GPT-5.5 Catalog Metadata (Pi 0.70.0)

Dependencies updated to Pi 0.70.0, adding GPT-5.5 to the model catalog. GPT-5.5 is now selectable as an agent model in your `openclaw.json` without manual catalog patching or custom provider entries.

```json
{
  "agents": {
    "defaults": {
      "model": "openai/gpt-5.5"
    }
  }
}
```

---

## 89 Bug Fixes: What Got Stabilized

The fix count in v2026.4.23 is notable. Key areas:

**Messaging channels (WhatsApp, Telegram, Discord, Slack)**
Integration stability improvements across all four channels. For SDR teams running simultaneous multi-channel campaigns, this reduces dropped messages and connection resets.

**Codex harness routing + context-engine assembly**
The harness selection logic — which model backend handles which request — now has structured debug logging and routing fixes. Misrouted requests under load are addressed.

**Security hardening**
Authentication, permissions, and data handling across the gateway have been tightened. Relevant for teams handling prospect PII over WhatsApp and Telegram.

**Plugin startup + dependency resolution**
Plugin initialization failures on first boot and after updates are fixed. The `auto-install` behavior from v2026.4.22 now completes reliably.

**Memory indexing + local embedding provider**
Edge cases in local embedding provider declaration and memory index updates are resolved. Directly improves recall accuracy for long-running SDR agents with large prospect memory stores.

---

## Upgrade Guide

### From v2026.4.22 (last release)

No breaking changes. Standard upgrade:

```bash
openclaw update
openclaw doctor
openclaw reload
```

### Enable Image Generation (OpenAI)

```json
{
  "providers": {
    "openai": {
      "tools": {
        "image_generation": {
          "enabled": true,
          "auth": "codex-oauth"
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

### Configure Subagents with Forked Context

```json
{
  "agents": {
    "sdr-qualifier": {
      "subagents": [
        {
          "id": "researcher",
          "contextMode": "forked",
          "systemPrompt": "You are a lead research specialist..."
        }
      ]
    }
  }
}
```

---

## Comparison: Manual SDR vs. AI SDR on OpenClaw v2026.4.23

| Capability | Manual SDR | AI SDR on OpenClaw v2026.4.23 |
|---|---|---|
| Proposal visuals | Design team request (days) | In-conversation image generation (seconds) |
| Multi-stage deal handling | Rep context-switches manually | Subagent forked context per stage |
| Media tool reliability | Global timeout applies | Per-call `timeoutMs` per tool |
| On-premise embedding | Cloud dependency | Local embeddings, configurable context size |
| GPT-5.5 access | Manual catalog patch | Native catalog entry |
| Channel stability | Varies by integration | 89 fixes across WhatsApp/TG/Slack/Discord |
| Setup time | Days | 5 minutes with `install.sh` |

---

## FAQ

**Do I need a Codex OAuth token to use image generation?**
Yes for OpenAI. The Codex OAuth flow is handled within the OpenClaw provider config — no separate application registration if you already have an OpenAI account with Codex access.

**Can subagents write back to the parent agent's memory?**
In forked context mode, subagent memory is isolated by default. You can configure explicit memory sync gates in the subagent handoff config to push selected facts back to the parent.

**Does the 89-fix count include security patches?**
Yes. Security hardening across authentication, permissions, and data handling is explicitly listed among the fixed areas. Upgrading is recommended for all production deployments.

**Can I use GPT-5.5 on OpenRouter instead of directly?**
Yes — OpenRouter now also supports image generation. If GPT-5.5 is available through OpenRouter's catalog, it will work through the OpenRouter provider config.

**What is the minimum hardware for local embeddings with contextSize 2048?**
The local embedding provider will run on 1 GB RAM with contextSize 2048. Default 4096 requires roughly 2 GB RAM depending on the embedding model loaded.
